# Lumina Retrieval & Generation Evaluation

*Generated by `make eval` on 2026-07-12T00:17:33.244115Z — frozen dataset of 42 questions over the documented corpus in `eval/corpus/`. Do not edit by hand.*

## Retrieval ablation

Each row adds one component. Metrics are means over all answerable questions; binary relevance against hand-verified ground-truth chunk ids.

| Configuration | recall@5 | recall@10 | MRR | NDCG@10 | p50 latency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| dense only (baseline) | 0.571 | 0.690 | 0.427 | 0.469 | 28 ms |
| sparse only (BM25) | 0.881 | 0.940 | 0.811 | 0.818 | 12 ms |
| hybrid RRF (BM25+dense) | 0.762 | 0.905 | 0.670 | 0.707 | 33 ms |
| hybrid RRF (FTS+dense) | 0.619 | 0.714 | 0.468 | 0.506 | 23 ms |
| hybrid + rerank | 0.869 | 0.905 | 0.795 | 0.793 | 347 ms |
| hybrid + rerank + multi-query | 0.869 | 0.905 | 0.795 | 0.793 | 1069 ms |

## Method notes

- Dataset: `eval/dataset.jsonl`, frozen; each item's `relevant_chunk_ids` were verified to contain the answer before freezing. Items were drafted by an LLM assistant from the actual chunk contents and checked against their source chunks.
- Corpus ingested deterministically (`make ingest`): sorted file order, sha256-derived document ids, recursive chunking (800/150).
- Retrieval runs call the production `RetrievalPipeline` in-process — the same code path the API serves, not a reimplementation.
- Sparse `bm25` rows use true Okapi BM25 (`rank_bm25`) computed in-process; `fts` rows use Postgres `ts_rank_cd` (not BM25) fused in a single SQL statement.
